fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A displayed morphism u\xrightarrow [1_{x}]{} v\in E^{\mathsf {op}} is determined by a span {\mathopen {}\left \{u\leftarrow v_{x} \to v\right \}\mathclose {}}\in E where the right-hand map is cartesian over 1_{x} : x\to x and the left-hand map is vertical, taken up to the identification of different cartesian lifts v_{x}\to x. A displayed morphism that is cartesian over the identity is an isomorphism; hence, displayed morphisms u\xrightarrow [1_{x}]{} v\in E^{\mathsf {op}} are equivalently determined by vertical maps v\to u \in E.

The construction of fibered opposite categories does appear quite involved, but it can be seen to be inevitable from the perspective of the fiber categories E^{\mathsf {op}}_{x}. Indeed, let u,v\in E^{\mathsf {op}}_{x} and fix a vertical map h : u \to v\in E^{\mathsf {op}}_{x}; by unfolding definitions, we see that the vertical map h : u \to v is uniquely determined by a morphism v\to u\in E_{x}.

A displayed morphism u\xrightarrow [1_{x}]{} v\in E^{\mathsf {op}} is determined by a span {\mathopen {}\left \{u\leftarrow v_{x} \to v\right \}\mathclose {}}\in E where the right-hand map is cartesian over 1_{x} : x\to x and the left-hand map is vertical, taken up to the identification of different cartesian lifts v_{x}\to x. A displayed morphism that is cartesian over the identity is an isomorphism; hence, displayed morphisms u\xrightarrow [1_{x}]{} v\in E^{\mathsf {op}} are equivalently determined by vertical maps v\to u \in E.
